name: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
description: The workplace comedy about Detective Jake Peralta and the NYPD's fictional 99th Precinct

questions.q: Which network originally premiered Brooklyn Nine-Nine in 2013?

source.notes: Premiered Sept 17, 2013 on Fox; later moved to NBC in 2019.

questions.q: Which actor plays Detective Jake Peralta?

source.notes: Samberg won the Golden Globe for Best Actor in a Comedy for Season 1.

questions.q: Who played the 99th Precinct's stoic commanding officer Captain Raymond Holt?

source.notes: Braugher earned four Emmy nominations for the role; he died in December 2023.

questions.q: What is the name of Jake Peralta's favorite movie, which he references constantly throughout the series?

source.notes: Jake models his detective persona on John McClane.

questions.q: What is the name of Captain Holt's beloved Pembroke Welsh Corgi?

source.notes: Holt's pride and joy, co-parented with husband Kevin Cozner.

questions.q: Which three-word catchphrase does Jake use to project nonchalant approval?

source.notes: Often extended to 'cool cool cool cool cool no doubt no doubt no doubt.'

questions.q: What annual competition pits the squad against each other for bragging rights and a customized belt?

source.notes: Recurring fan-favorite episode format across seven seasons.

questions.q: After Fox canceled Brooklyn Nine-Nine in 2018, which network picked it up for its final three seasons?

source.notes: Fan outcry drove NBC to pick it up within 31 hours of the cancellation.

questions.q: How many seasons of Brooklyn Nine-Nine aired in total?

source.notes: 153 episodes total; series finale aired September 2021.

questions.q: Which writing duo co-created Brooklyn Nine-Nine after previously working together on Parks and Recreation?

source.notes: Schur also co-created The Office (US adaptation) and The Good Place.

questions.q: Which actress plays the tough, secretive Detective Rosa Diaz?

source.notes: Beatriz originally auditioned for Amy Santiago.

questions.q: What is the name of Charles Boyle's adopted son, introduced in Season 4?

source.notes: Adopted from Latvia; pronounced with theatrical emphasis by Boyle.

questions.q: Which veteran actress recurred as Holt's scheming rival Deputy Commissioner Madeline Wuntch?

source.notes: Wuntch appeared across multiple seasons until her character's death in Season 8.

questions.q: What do Jake and Amy name their son, a nod to Die Hard's John McClane?

source.notes: Son introduced in Season 8; name is a direct Die Hard reference.

questions.q: Who plays Jake's recurring nemesis-turned-friend, the charming car thief Doug Judy (the Pontiac Bandit)?

source.notes: Robinson appeared in a Pontiac Bandit episode nearly every season.

questions.q: Which comedian played Rosa's volatile, on-again-off-again fiancé Adrian Pimento?

source.notes: Pimento was a deep-undercover detective with lingering paranoia.

questions.q: In which season's finale did Jake and Amy get married at the 99th Precinct?

source.notes: A bomb threat forced the ceremony to move outside the precinct.

questions.q: What is the name of Captain Holt's husband, a literature professor?

source.notes: Played by Marc Evan Jackson; Columbia University professor of Classics.

questions.q: Which pair of lazy, desk-bound senior detectives share their surnames with Parks and Recreation writing staff?

source.notes: Named after Parks and Rec writers Norm Hiscock and Mike Scully.

questions.q: What fictional potato chip brand shows up on Boyle's desk and across other sitcoms as a running prop gag?

source.notes: Prop brand appearing in Seinfeld, New Girl, Community, and others.

questions.q: On what date did the final season of Brooklyn Nine-Nine premiere on NBC?

source.notes: Delayed from 2020 due to COVID-19 and scripts rewritten after the George Floyd protests.

questions.q: Terry Jeffords is famously obsessed with which dairy product?

source.notes: Recurring gag; Terry's office fridge is stocked with it.
